title: Survival factors for hPSC growth

steps: Select survival factor according to the desired culture condition or lab-workflow from the dropdown below. Presence of survival factors in the culture medium can have strong effects on hPSC morphology. Refer to Reference pictures of hPSC cultured in defined conditions for examples of typical morphologies.
Prepare 10,000x stock solutions for each compound:

Prepare 1 mL of 1000 X CET ( C hroman 1, E mricasan, T rans-ISRIB) cocktail as described in Table 4 . Prepare 1 mL 1000x CET according to the table below, make 10 µL aliquots and store at -80 o C. Add 10 µL CET cocktail to 10 mL hPSC medium followed by 10 µL 1000x Polyamine Solutions. %s %s
Spin down the vial containing ROCK inhibitor Y-27632 (ROCKi) powder prior to opening.

Reconstitute in required volume of sterile water in a 15 mL Falcon tube. Refer to Table 1 for quantity and corresponding volume. %s %s Alternatively, use DMSO as solvent. ROCKi solubility in both water and DMSO is maximum 100 millimolar (mM)
Prepare 200 µL aliquots in 0.5 mL eppendorf tubes. If ROCKi is provided as ready-to-use solution e.g. 10 millimolar (mM) , prepare 50 µL, - 100 µL aliquots and store as described below.
Store aliquots at -20 °C or at -80 °C for long-term storage (up to two years).
Thaw required number of ROCKi aliquots according to culture media volume needed. After thawing, ROCKi aliquots can be stored at 4 °C for up to two weeks. Do not repeatedly freeze-thaw this reagent.
Supplement culture media to the final concentration of 10 micromolar (µM) ROCKi ( Table 2 ). %s %s
Thaw CloneR2 at Room temperature .

Suppplement culture media with CloneR2 using 1:10 or 1:20 dilution factor. Refer to Table 6 for various volumes. A B C D E 1 Final culture media volume for 10 mL for 25 mL for 50 mL for 100 mL 2 CloneR2 (mL for 1:10 or 1:20 dilutions) 1 or 0.5 2.5 or 1.25 5 or 2.5 10 or 5 Table 6. Volume of CloneR2 according to different dilutions and final supplemented culture media volume. Manufacturer recommends a working dilution of 1:10. We have tested side by side and 1:20 dilution is working similarly. However, we cannot discard hPSC lines different outcomes. Do take into account to use a lower dilution when needed.

Supplement an aliquot of complete hPSC culture medium with small molecules prepared in Step 2 using 1:10,000 dilution factor. Supplement same aliquot of complete hPSC culture medium with ready-to-use Polyamine solution using 1:1,000 dilution factor. Refer to Table 3 for volumes. A B C D E 1 Compound Stock Concentration Final concentration Volume for 50 mL culture media Volume for 100 mL culture media 2 Chroman 1 0.5 mM 50 nM 5 µL 10 µL 3 Emricasan 50 mM 5 µM 5 µL 10 µL 4 Trans-ISRIB 7 mM 0.7 µM 5 µL 10 µL 5 Polyamine Solution 1000 X 1 X 50 µL 100 µL Table 3. Culture media supplementation with CEPT using individual aliquots.

Always supplement fresh the culture media with ROCKi prior to use. Proceed with cell culture, refer to protocols: Maintenance of hPSC Single cell passaging of hPSC Reference pictures of hPSC cultured in defined conditions

Always supplement fresh the culture media with CEPT prior to use. Proceed with cell culture, refer to protocols: Maintenance of hPSC Single cell passaging of hPSC Reference pictures of hPSC cultured in defined conditions

Chroman 1 - Stock concentration 0.5 millimolar (mM) 1. Reconstitute 5 mg Chroman 1 in 22.91 mL DMSO. 2. Transfer to a 50 mL conical tube by rinsing with increasing volumes of DMSO. 3. Mix thoroughly until complete dissolution using Vortex. 4. Prepare 100 µL aliquots. 5. Store at -80 °C . Avoid repetitive freeze-thaw cycles.

Emricasan - Stock concentration 50 millimolar (mM) 1. Reconstitute 10 mg Emricasan in 351 µL DMSO in the original vial. 2. Mix thoroughly until complete dissolution using Vortex. 3. Prepare 50 µL aliquots. 4. Store at -80 °C . Avoid repetitive freeze-thaw cycles.

Trans-ISRIB - Stock concentration 7 millimolar (mM) 1. Reconstitute 10 mg Trans-ISRIB in 3.165 mL DMSO in a 15mL conical tube. 2. Transfer to a 15 mL conical tube by rinsing with increasing volumes of DMSO. 3. Mix thoroughly until complete dissolution using Vortex. 4. Prepare 100 µL aliquots. 5. Store at -80 °C . Avoid repetitive freeze-thaw cycles.
Prepare 10 µL to 100 µL aliquots of CET and store at -80 °C . Avoid repetitive freeze-thaw cycles.

Supplement an aliquot of complete hPSC culture medium with CET cocktail using 1:1,000 dilution factor. Supplement same aliquot of complete hPSC culture medium with ready-to-use Polyamine solution using 1:1,000 dilution factor. Refer to Table 5 for volumes. A B C D E 1 Compound Stock Concentration Final concentration Volume for 50 mL culture media Volume for 100 mL culture media 2 CET 1000 X 1 X 50 µL 100 µL 3 Polyamine Solution 1000 X 1 X 50 µL 100 µL Table 5. Culture media supplementation with CEPT using CET cocktail + Polyamine.

Prepare 1 mL aliquots of CloneR2 and store at -20 °C . Avoid repetitive freeze-thaw cycles. Once aliquots are thawed, use immediately. Remaining aliquot content can be stored for up to 1 week at 4 °C .
Mix the solution thoroughly by inverting. Avoid shaking, as this may cause foam formation.

Always supplement fresh the culture media with CloneR2 prior to use. Proceed with cell culture, refer to protocols: Maintenance of hPSC Single cell passaging of hPSC Reference pictures of hPSC cultured in defined conditions
